message:
fix(net): guard pool state transitions, use deadline-relative connect timeouts, always report TimedOut on overall timeout

HTTP connection pool (pool.rs):
- Add mark_connected() guard: only transitions Connecting → Idle,
  returning false for any other state. Previously mark_connected on
  an InUse connection would silently reset it to Idle, corrupting the
  pool's active-connection count.
- Add release() guard: only transitions InUse → Idle, returning false
  for Connecting/Unhealthy/Idle states. Previously releasing a
  Connecting or Unhealthy connection would mark it Idle and make it
  available for checkout despite never having completed handshake or
  being known-broken.
- Pool::mark_connected and Pool::release now delegate to the guarded
  methods. Added tests: release on Connecting is no-op, mark_connected
  on InUse is no-op, release on Unhealthy is no-op.

DNS module docs (dns/mod.rs):
- Update module-level documentation to reflect the current
  implementation (system-resolver fast path for default config,
  custom transport for explicit nameservers), replacing the outdated
  "Phase 0" language.

Happy Eyeballs (happy_eyeballs.rs):
- Extract finish_overall_timeout() to always return a TimedOut error
  with the canonical OVERALL_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT_MSG. Previously, when
  the overall timeout expired, the code returned last_error if one
  existed — meaning a ConnectionRefused from an early attempt could
  mask the fact that the operation actually timed out, confusing
  callers who check error kind.
- Added test: timeout overrides prior connect error, confirming the
  returned error is TimedOut (not ConnectionRefused).

TCP connect (tcp/stream.rs):
- Compute a single deadline at the start and derive per-attempt
  remaining time from it. Previously each attempt got the full
  timeout_duration, so N addresses × T timeout = N×T total wait,
  far exceeding the caller's requested timeout.
- Break immediately on timeout instead of continuing to the next
  address with zero remaining time.
- Added test: connect times out before the first attempt when the
  time getter advances past the deadline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
